tossups.question: Emanuel Leutze created six paintings depicting this man’s life, including one in which he stands before two rulers as a monk and a boy unveil a chart and a flag behind him. For an exhibition subtitled for this person and “wohs” (woes), Jaune Quick-to-See Smith created a collage titled Trade featuring a canoe with a clothesline of sports merchandise above it. (*) Edward Sorin commissioned Luigi Gregori to paint 12 murals depicting this man’s life at the Main Building of Notre Dame. Although there are no contemporary portraits of this man, the most famous posthumous portrait was created by (*) Sebastiano del Piombo. A large surrealist painting titled for this man unusually features the Catalan flag and references the spears in The Surrender of Breda with numerous tall crucifixes that dominate the right side. In that painting, this man holds a banner that features the artist’s wife Gala as the Virgin Mary as he drags a boat up a beach. For 10 points, name this explorer whom Salvador Dalí depicted during his “Discovery of America.”
tossups.question_sanitized: Emanuel Leutze created six paintings depicting this man's life, including one in which he stands before two rulers as a monk and a boy unveil a chart and a flag behind him. For an exhibition subtitled for this person and "wohs" (woes), Jaune Quick-to-See Smith created a collage titled Trade featuring a canoe with a clothesline of sports merchandise above it. (*) Edward Sorin commissioned Luigi Gregori to paint 12 murals depicting this man's life at the Main Building of Notre Dame. Although there are no contemporary portraits of this man, the most famous posthumous portrait was created by (*) Sebastiano del Piombo. A large surrealist painting titled for this man unusually features the Catalan flag and references the spears in The Surrender of Breda with numerous tall crucifixes that dominate the right side. In that painting, this man holds a banner that features the artist's wife Gala as the Virgin Mary as he drags a boat up a beach. For 10 points, name this explorer whom Salvador Dali depicted during his "Discovery of America."

tossups.question: An “itch” of this many years titles a photograph of Shelley Niro lounging on the trunk of her car. In a piece commenting on a period of this many years, two artists wore leopard prints and claimed to be from the island of Guatinau. For a three-year period centering on an anniversary of this number, James Luna told passersby at D.C.’s Union Station to “take a picture” with him. Public commemoration of an anniversary of this many years prompted Guillermo Gómez-Peña and (*) Coco Fusco’s Couple in a Cage, as well as a “non-celebration” piece in which sports caps dangle from a clothesline above a painting of a canoe, Jaune Quick-To-See Smith’s Trade. For 10 points, Indigenous People’s Day was founded on what anniversary of Columbus’s arrival in America?
tossups.question_sanitized: An "itch" of this many years titles a photograph of Shelley Niro lounging on the trunk of her car. In a piece commenting on a period of this many years, two artists wore leopard prints and claimed to be from the island of Guatinau. For a three-year period centering on an anniversary of this number, James Luna told passersby at D.C.'s Union Station to "take a picture" with him. Public commemoration of an anniversary of this many years prompted Guillermo Gomez-Pena and (*) Coco Fusco's Couple in a Cage, as well as a "non-celebration" piece in which sports caps dangle from a clothesline above a painting of a canoe, Jaune Quick-To-See Smith's Trade. For 10 points, Indigenous People's Day was founded on what anniversary of Columbus's arrival in America?
tossups.answer: 500 years [accept Columbus Quincentenary or quincentennial; accept 500 Year Itch, “500 Years of Resistance,” or “The Quincentenary Non-Celebration”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: 500 years [accept Columbus Quincentenary or quincentennial; accept 500 Year Itch, "500 Years of Resistance," or "The Quincentenary Non-Celebration"]

set.name: 2023 "Questions Based on Wikipedia Links Ophir Sent Me"

tossups.question: Native American artist Jaune Quick-to-See Smith sketched an outline of this work over newsprint and placed a large red circle with an X over it in her The Red Mean. In 2011, H. Ashrafian argued that the swelling in the groin of this work's namesake figure indicates a hernia. It influenced William Blake's Albion Rose, though it may itself have been a derivative of a sketch by Giacomo Andrea de Ferrara. While the torso of this work's central figure faces forward, his legs slant toward his left so that one foot is in profile. Sharing similarities with Le Corbusier's (*) modulor system, this work contains paragraphs of notes above and below the central figure with maxims like “6 palms make 1 cubit.” The navel of this work's title figure forms the center of a circle that is tangent to a square at two of his four feet, while the square itself is defined by the wingspan of his outstretched arms. For 10 points, name this sketch of ideal human proportions by Leonardo da Vinci.
tossups.question_sanitized: Native American artist Jaune Quick-to-See Smith sketched an outline of this work over newsprint and placed a large red circle with an X over it in her The Red Mean. In 2011, H. Ashrafian argued that the swelling in the groin of this work's namesake figure indicates a hernia. It influenced William Blake's Albion Rose, though it may itself have been a derivative of a sketch by Giacomo Andrea de Ferrara. While the torso of this work's central figure faces forward, his legs slant toward his left so that one foot is in profile. Sharing similarities with Le Corbusier's (*) modulor system, this work contains paragraphs of notes above and below the central figure with maxims like "6 palms make 1 cubit." The navel of this work's title figure forms the center of a circle that is tangent to a square at two of his four feet, while the square itself is defined by the wingspan of his outstretched arms. For 10 points, name this sketch of ideal human proportions by Leonardo da Vinci.

bonuses.leadin: One of these items is attached to the top of an eleven-foot-long artwork created on the 500th anniversary of Christopher Columbus’s arrival in the Americas. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: One of these items is attached to the top of an eleven-foot-long artwork created on the 500th anniversary of Christopher Columbus's arrival in the Americas. For 10 points each:
parts: What kind of item subtitles a 1992 collage by Jaune Quick-to-See Smith? A blue-and-yellow one of these items occupies a red field in a 1955 encaustic painting featuring plaster casts of four faces above it.
This American Pop artist of Target with Four Faces also created many other encaustic paintings of maps and American flags.

This artist used a target for the head of her assemblage Saint Sébastien. This French sculptor, known for her monumental bulbous and colorful figures, created a Tarot Garden as well as the bird-like Sun God at UCSD.
parts_sanitized: What kind of item subtitles a 1992 collage by Jaune Quick-to-See Smith? A blue-and-yellow one of these items occupies a red field in a 1955 encaustic painting featuring plaster casts of four faces above it.
This American Pop artist of Target with Four Faces also created many other encaustic paintings of maps and American flags.

This artist used a target for the head of her assemblage Saint Sebastien. This French sculptor, known for her monumental bulbous and colorful figures, created a Tarot Garden as well as the bird-like Sun God at UCSD.
answers: targets [accept dartboards or bull’s eyes; accept I See Red: Target; accept Target with Four Faces; prompt on concentric circles]
Niki de Saint Phalle [or Catherine Marie-Agnès Fal de Saint Phalle]
answers_sanitized: targets [accept dartboards or bull's eyes; accept I See Red: Target; accept Target with Four Faces; prompt on concentric circles]
Niki de Saint Phalle [or Catherine Marie-Agnes Fal de Saint Phalle]

bonuses.leadin: A series by this artist inspired Jaune Quick-to-See Smith to attach one of its subjects at the top of I See Red, her assemblage showing historic representations of Native Americans. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A series by this artist inspired Jaune Quick-to-See Smith to attach one of its subjects at the top of I See Red, her assemblage showing historic representations of Native Americans. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist. In 1955, this artist began a series that used encaustic painting on newspaper to create circular targets, which were often accompanied with items like plaster casts and watercolor cakes.

Targets were a recurring theme in the artworks of Kenneth Noland and Alma Thomas, who belonged to a “Color School” movement based in this city. This city is home to the Smithsonian’s Hirshhorn Museum.
This artist drew on Johns and Noland’s target motif for his painting The First Real Target. With Jann Haworth, this British pop artist designed the cover of Sgt. Pepper’s Lonely Hearts Club Band.
parts_sanitized: Name this artist. In 1955, this artist began a series that used encaustic painting on newspaper to create circular targets, which were often accompanied with items like plaster casts and watercolor cakes.

Targets were a recurring theme in the artworks of Kenneth Noland and Alma Thomas, who belonged to a "Color School" movement based in this city. This city is home to the Smithsonian's Hirshhorn Museum.
This artist drew on Johns and Noland's target motif for his painting The First Real Target. With Jann Haworth, this British pop artist designed the cover of Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band.
answers_sanitized: Washington, D.C. [or Washington, D.C.; or District of Columbia]
Peter Blake [or Sir Peter Thomas Blake]

bonuses.leadin: In 2019, a painting by this artist depicting three Native Americans watching railroad construction was exhibited with artworks by indigenous artists like Jaune Quick-to-See Smith. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist of Progress. Jonathan Sturges commissioned a painting by this artist depicting a recently deceased man and referencing a eulogy based on John Keats's sonnet "O Solitude."
In 2005, Alice Walton purchased this Asher Durand painting depicting another painter and the poet William Cullen Bryant standing on a ledge in the Catskill Mountains.

Durand wrote that "if there be a man on earth whose location I envy… it is" this Catskills native who appears with Bryant in Kindred Spirits. This founder of the Hudson River School painted The Oxbow.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In 2019, a painting by this artist depicting three Native Americans watching railroad construction was exhibited with artworks by indigenous artists like Jaune Quick-to-See Smith. For 10 points each:

bonuses.leadin: On a centenary of this event, Jaune Quick-to-See Smith created a collage topped by a clothesline of sports memorabilia entitled Trade. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this event commemorated by a painting in which the artist appears as a monk and his wife appears as the Virgin Mary on a large white banner. Much of that painting of this event is filled with improbably lengthy crosses.
This Spanish artist of The Discovery of America by Christopher Columbus painted melting clocks in his surreal The Persistence of Memory.

This artist used his engraving “Columbus Breaking the Egg” to promote his book The Analysis of Beauty. Young comforts a half-naked man in a painting by him in which fashionably-dressed women tour an insane asylum.
answers: Columbus's "discovery" of America [or obvious equivalents indicating Columbus arriving in America or the New World; prompt on answers like “Columbus's voyage”]
answers_sanitized: Columbus's "discovery" of America [or obvious equivalents indicating Columbus arriving in America or the New World; prompt on answers like "Columbus's voyage"]
